body:
Title: CRA Form T2203 2025 Provincial and Territorial Taxes for Multiple Jurisdictions.

Source:
- https://www.canada.ca/content/dam/cra-arc/formspubs/pbg/t2203/t2203-25e.pdf

CRA T2203 2025 is used to calculate provincial and territorial taxes for multiple jurisdictions. Normalized numeric values used by the encoded formulas include 0.48 and 0.165. Part 1 line 1 uses net income from line 23600 and the deduction for split income claimed on line 23200. Line 2 is the total of lines 12200, 13500, 13700, 13900, 14100, and 14300, excluding losses. Line 3 subtracts line 2 from line 1 and floors negative amounts at zero. Part 1 column 4 adds columns 2 and 3 if line 1 is more than line 2 or the taxpayer is a non-resident. If the taxpayer is a resident of Canada and line 1 is less than line 2, column 4 prorates line 1 by each jurisdiction's column 2 percentage. Column 5 uses income allocated in column 4 to determine the percentage for each jurisdiction. Quebec is line 52140 and Other Outside Canada is line 52220.

Part 2 line 6 enters whichever is more: basic federal tax line 42900 or T1206 line 22 tax on split income. Line 8 multiplies line 6 by the percentage allocated to Other Outside Canada. Line 9 is the federal surtax rate of 48%, encoded as 0.48. Line 10 multiplies line 8 by line 9, or uses Form T691 when minimum tax applies. Line 11 uses the greater of basic federal tax or tax on split income, or T691 Part 6 line 7 when minimum tax applies. Line 13 multiplies line 11 by the percentage allocated to Quebec. Line 14 is the refundable Quebec abatement rate of 16.5%, encoded as 0.165. Line 15 multiplies line 13 by line 14 and is entered on line 44000. Part 5 line 13 adds the amounts from the provincial and territorial Part 4 forms and enters total provincial and territorial taxes on line 42800.
